assertions.expectation: Identifies this as an admission webhook compatibility issue — the webhook is rejecting pod creation requests
assertions.reason: Response clearly identifies this as 'a common issue during Kubernetes upgrades where admission webhooks become incompatible or unavailable' and explains webhooks are blocking pod creation

assertions.expectation: Provides commands to identify the problematic webhook (kubectl get validatingwebhookconfigurations / mutatingwebhookconfigurations)
assertions.reason: Provides exact commands: 'kubectl get validatingadmissionwebhooks' and 'kubectl get mutatingadmissionwebhooks'

assertions.expectation: Offers an immediate fix: temporarily disable or remove the problematic webhook
assertions.reason: Explicitly offers 'Delete the Problematic Webhook (Fastest)' as Option 1 for immediate fix

assertions.expectation: Provides the kubectl delete command for the webhook configuration as emergency mitigation
assertions.reason: Provides specific delete commands: 'kubectl delete validatingadmissionwebhook cert-manager-webhook' and 'kubectl delete mutatingadmissionwebhook cert-manager-webhook'

assertions.expectation: Warns that deleting the webhook removes validation — it's a temporary fix
assertions.reason: While it mentions this is for 'temporarily' removing webhooks, it doesn't explicitly warn that this removes validation capabilities or explain the security implications

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking cert-manager version compatibility with Kubernetes 1.32
assertions.reason: Includes section 'Check cert-manager compatibility' with command to check cert-manager version and explicitly mentions upgrading 'to compatible version for k8s 1.32'

assertions.expectation: Advises upgrading cert-manager to a version that supports 1.30 as the permanent fix
assertions.reason: Provides detailed upgrade instructions using helm and kubectl apply methods, recommending v1.16.0 as 'latest compatible version'

assertions.expectation: Mentions setting failurePolicy to Ignore as a less drastic alternative to deletion
assertions.reason: Option 2 explicitly shows editing webhook to add 'failurePolicy: Ignore' as alternative approach

assertions.expectation: Includes commands to verify pods can be created after the fix
assertions.reason: Verify Fix section includes 'kubectl run test-pod --image=nginx --rm -it --restart=Never' to test pod creation

assertions.expectation: Notes that any webhook configuration changes (failurePolicy, cert-manager version) must be persisted in the source of truth (Helm chart, GitOps manifests, CI/CD pipeline) to survive redeployment
assertions.reason: Response doesn't mention the need to persist changes in source of truth like Helm charts, GitOps manifests, or CI/CD pipelines

assertions.expectation: Qualifies failurePolicy: Ignore as a temporary debugging measure with explicit risks, recommending revert to Fail after the root cause is resolved
assertions.reason: While it mentions failurePolicy: Ignore option, it doesn't explicitly qualify it as temporary debugging measure with risks or recommend reverting to Fail after resolution
